prediction.summary: A credential stuffing attack, characterized by distributed login attempts from multiple source IPs, targeted the 'user@corp' account. The adversary successfully authenticated to both the web application and SSH service using compromised credentials from IP 198.51.100.123. An internal audit system confirmed the distributed credential reuse, indicating a successful account compromise.

Audit system detected distributed credential reuse across 8 source IPs for account user@corp

recommendations: Immediately disable the 'user@corp' account and force a password reset for the user.
Terminate all active sessions associated with 'user@corp' on affected systems (web and SSH).
Block the source IP address 198.51.100.123 at the network perimeter (firewall/security group).
Investigate post-login activity for 'user@corp' on the 'web' server to identify any commands executed, data accessed, or lateral movement attempts.
Review MFA logs for 'user@corp' to determine if MFA was bypassed or if the account was not protected by MFA.
Analyze logs for other accounts for similar distributed login attempts to identify if other accounts were also targeted or compromised.
